title: Dolichoglottis scorzoneroides

The classification of this plant is further supported by its characteristics, such as its leaf structure and growth habits, which are distinct from those of other related species. intro: Dolichoglottis scorzoneroides, also known as the New Zealand lettuce daisy or scorzonera lettuce daisy, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in New Zealand for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions. Bioactive constituents of Dolichoglottis scorzoneroides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
